message:
feat(core): MCP TOON output format, runtime scheduling, and engine hardening

Introduce TOON (a compact binary-safe encoding) as an alternative MCP
output format alongside JSON, add snapshot trigger scheduling to the
runtime, and harden several core subsystems with cleanup logic, tests,
and benchmarks.

MCP output format (mcp.rs, +324 lines):
  - Add McpOutputFormat enum with Json and Toon variants
  - parse_mcp_output_format() for string→enum conversion with fallback
  - extract_mcp_output_format() to pull format from serde_json params
  - Integration with the toon_rust crate behind mcp-server feature flag
  - Roundtrip tests verifying JSON↔TOON encoding equivalence

Runtime scheduling (runtime.rs, +339 lines):
  - SnapshotSchedulingMode enum: Periodic, EventDriven, Adaptive
  - SnapshotTrigger config struct with interval, backoff, and jitter
  - schedule_next_snapshot() computes next trigger time from mode/state
  - Adaptive mode adjusts frequency based on change-rate heuristics
  - Comprehensive unit tests for each scheduling mode and edge cases

Unix socket infrastructure (runtime_compat.rs, +90 lines):
  - Platform-abstracted bind/connect/buffered/lines helpers
  - Gated behind asupersync-runtime feature for async socket scenarios

Native event cleanup (native_events.rs, +134 lines):
  - maybe_cleanup_stale_socket() detects and removes dead Unix sockets
  - Drop impl for NativeEventListener ensures socket cleanup on teardown
  - Unix-specific socket detection using file metadata and connect probes

Vendored mux refinements:
  - mux_client.rs (+175): extract resolve_compression_mode_for_locality()
    from resolve_compression_mode(), add decode_u64_leb128_prefix helper
    with unit tests for compression locality decisions
  - mux_pool.rs (+3): move timeout import to test module scope
  - wire_protocol.rs (+33): track rejected messages via total_rejected
    counter in Aggregator::ingest(), add rejection counting test

Build and benchmarks:
  - Cargo.toml: add toon_rust optional dep, compression_bypass and
    shard_routing bench entries, update mcp-server feature deps
  - compression_bypass.rs (293 lines, new): benchmarks for compression
    bypass path performance, gated on vendored feature
  - storage_regression.rs (+270): aggregator merge benchmarks with CI
    budget annotations for regression detection
  - topology_serialization.rs (+254): TOON-vs-JSON encode/decode
    benchmarks comparing serialization throughput
  - asupersync_unix_socket.rs (131 lines, new): integration tests for
    Unix socket bind/connect lifecycle (#![cfg(unix)])
  - distributed_merge_dpor.rs (276 lines, new): DPOR-style distributed
    merge tests for concurrent aggregator scenarios
  - docs/mcp-api-spec.md: document format parameter ("json"|"toon")

Miscellaneous:
  - tailer.rs: remove unused CapturedSegmentKind import

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -66,6 +66,7 @@ ftui = { workspace = true, optional = true }
 
 # MCP server (optional, behind feature)
 fastmcp = { workspace = true, optional = true }
+toon_rust = { workspace = true, optional = true }
 # Web server (optional, behind feature)
 fastapi = { workspace = true, optional = true }
 fastapi-core = { workspace = true, optional = true }
@@ -162,6 +163,15 @@ name = "pdu_pipelining"
 harness = false
 required-features = ["vendored"]
 
+[[bench]]
+name = "compression_bypass"
+harness = false
+required-features = ["vendored"]
+
+[[bench]]
+name = "shard_routing"
+harness = false
+
 [[bench]]
 name = "bocpd"
 harness = false
@@ -264,4 +274,4 @@ semantic-search = ["dep:tokenizers", "dep:safetensors", "dep:half", "dep:fastemb
 frankenterm-deps = ["dep:codec", "dep:config", "dep:mux", "dep:frankenterm-term"]
 vendored-wezterm = ["frankenterm-deps"]  # backward compat alias
 browser-automation = []
-mcp-server = ["dep:fastmcp"]
+mcp-server = ["dep:fastmcp", "dep:toon_rust"]

@@ -24,7 +24,7 @@ ft mcp serve
 
 ## Response Envelope (v1)
 
-All MCP tool calls return the same envelope:
+By default (`format=json`), MCP tool calls return this JSON envelope:
 
 ```json
 {
@@ -44,6 +44,9 @@ Notes:
 - When `ok=false`, `data` is omitted and `error` is populated.
 - `data` MUST match the corresponding robot JSON schema under `docs/json-schema/`.
 - `now` is epoch milliseconds.
+- Tools also accept optional `format: "json" | "toon"` in params:
+  - `format=json` (default): response text is JSON envelope as shown above.
+  - `format=toon`: response text is TOON-encoded envelope with the same logical fields.
 
 ## Tool List (v1)
 
@@ -82,6 +85,7 @@ legacy `wa://...` scheme for backward compatibility.
 ### Tool Params (v1)
 
 Parameter types use JSON primitives; `u64` fields are JSON numbers.
+All tools accept an optional `format?: "json" | "toon"` parameter (default: `json`).
 
 - `wa.state`
   - Params: `{ domain?: string, agent?: string, pane_id?: u64 }`
